desc: Paphos blends archaeological sites, resort beaches, and low-key harbor life on Cyprus's southwest coast. It works well for travelers who want sun and sea plus easy access to ancient mosaics, sea caves, and wine villages in the Troodos foothills.
hls: UNESCO mosaics and ruins at Kato Paphos Archaeological Park
Harbor promenade with castles, tavernas, and sunset views
Sea caves, Akamas Peninsula trails, and Blue Lagoon boat trips
Mild winter climate compared with much of Europe
Reliable base for beach-and-history hybrid trips
Direct Gatwick flights in many seasons

todo.name: Kato Paphos Archaeological Park
todo.desc: Tour Roman villas and intricate mosaics, including the Houses of Dionysos and Theseus.

todo.name: Paphos Castle and harbor walk
todo.desc: Stroll the seafront, climb the small fortress, and watch sunset over the marina.

todo.name: Tombs of the Kings
todo.desc: Explore Hellenistic and Roman-era rock-cut tombs north of the city center.

todo.name: Coral Bay swim day
todo.desc: Spend a half day at one of the area's easiest sandy beaches with calm, swimmable water.

todo.name: Akamas Peninsula jeep or boat tour
todo.desc: Visit sea caves, coastal viewpoints, and the Blue Lagoon on a guided day outing.

todo.name: Aphrodite's Rock stop
todo.desc: Pause at Petra tou Romiou, linked to mythic origins of Aphrodite, for photos and a short coastal walk.

todo.name: Old Town (Ktima) food evening
todo.desc: Try meze, halloumi dishes, and Cypriot wines in a less touristy hilltop district.

todo.name: Troodos winery village loop
todo.desc: Drive inland to mountain villages such as Omodos for cellar tastings and traditional lanes.

wifi.note: Generally dependable in hotels and aparthotels; many beachfront venues also provide stable backup connections.

prac.fltNote: London Gatwick has regular nonstop service to Paphos (PFO), with strongest frequency in spring through autumn.
prac.lang: Greek, English widely used in tourism
prac.visa: Cyprus is in the EU but not Schengen; many non-EU visitors (including UK/US) can visit visa-free for short stays (commonly up to 90 days).
alerts: Some archaeological zones have limited shade; carry water and sun protection from late spring onward.
Car hire is useful for Akamas and mountain villages where public transport is sparse.

sweet: April to June and September to November give warm sea temperatures and lower resort crowd levels than peak July–August.
